mnemonics: STRINGS: Substring, Trim, Regex, Index, 'g' for global, Split - the core functions for manipulating text in R.
keyPoints: Use the stringr package (part of the tidyverse) for a consistent and powerful set of string functions.
Remember that R string functions are vectorized, meaning they operate on each element of a character vector.
Regular expressions (regex) are a powerful tool for pattern matching and complex string manipulation.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that R strings are case-sensitive (e.g., 'Data' != 'data') when doing comparisons or pattern matching.
Misusing fixed() in stringr functions when you intend to use a regex pattern, or vice-versa, leading to failed matches.
